_comment_goos: Go's stdlib has no windows/riscv64 combination, so we use goos=linux to keep TinyGo's runtime happy and rely on the llvm-target / linker steps to produce PE32+ output. Functionally this is fine: TinyGo with gc=leaking + scheduler=none + libc="" never touches libc or syscalls.
_comment_llvm_triple: riscv64-pc-windows-gnu makes LLVM emit COFF/PE relocations rather than ELF, which is what lld-link expects when stitching the final BOOTRISCV64.EFI. compiler-rt builds because the MinGW define path is taken.
